_comment: OpenCode project config emitted verbatim into .opencode/opencode.json by
gen/generate.py. The `instructions` array is the OpenCode analogue of the
Claude `@WORKFLOW.md` import: it lists the always-on core file(s) every
session loads. Per https://opencode.ai/docs/config/ the array accepts
relative/glob paths, so the submodule-relative path to the protocol's
always-on core is expressible here. There is NO in-file @-import in an
Read-on-demand topic-file split is re-expressed as this config entry plus
the harness-agnostic Read-on-demand pattern the protocol already uses for
PREVIEW: OpenCode is not yet enforcement-certified — see AGENTS.md for the
supported-harness declaration and the two named upstream gaps. The
enforcement plugin (tool.execute.before deny-list) is a LATER slice, gated
on Spike B; it is intentionally absent here.
